title: pyrola picta subsp. integra
intro: Pyrola picta subsp. integra, also known as Western Wintergreen, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is a perennial herb with oval-shaped leaves and white or pink flowers, commonly found in shaded, moist areas of the western United States. Western Wintergreen contains methyl salicylate, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which has been used to treat various health issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has been traditionally used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and soothe respiratory issues, as well as promote wound healing and digestive health.
